Ohtani will still DH for L.A. ...Read more
Shohei Ohtani's most impressive two-way season to date won't feature an All-Star Game appearance, the Los Angeles Dodgers announced Friday.
The 32-year-old Japanese standout is dealing with "continued irritation" in his left knee that will keep him off the mound Friday night.
"Unfortunately, due to these events, he will not be able to travel to Philadelphia and participate in the 2026 All-Star Game," the Dodgers said in their statement.
While Ohtani was unlikely to pitch in the Midsummer Classic, he was voted to start at designated hitter for the National League.
Earlier this week, the four-time league MVP and two-time World Series champion deposited his 300th career MLB big fly.
He's coming into the series against the D-backs ranked sixth in OPS (.939) and second in WAR (5.6) among all MLB players this season.
Most notably, Ohtani was sidelined in June because of left knee inflammation."},{"url":"https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7446438/2026/07/14/american-league-wins-mlb-all-star-2026/","title":"American League dominates in an All-Star Game both anticlimactic and uncertain - The Athletic","content":"3 days ago — Los Angeles Dodgers two-way star Shohei Ohtani was dealing with a knee issue. New York Yankees outfielder Aaron Judge has been sidelined ...Read more
The fans at Citizens Bank Park rode out the doldrums of a 4-0 American League victory in the 96th MLB All-Star Game because they knew Bryce Harper resided on the bench of the National League, and for as long as his presence lingered, so did the possibility of magic.
The moment arrived in the bottom of the sixth inning, when Los Angeles Dodgers manager Dave Roberts activated Harper, the two-time MVP and face of the Phillies franchise.
New York Yankees outfielder Aaron Judge has been sidelined since May with injured ribs.
Another potential calamity surfaced on Tuesday when Tampa Bay Rays star Junior Caminero left the game after getting hit by a pitch in the third inning; an X-ray on his hand came back clean, allowing the night to pass without controversy.
He visited Caminero in the American League clubhouse after Caminero received the good news.
The National League did not produce a base runner until New York Mets outfielder Juan Soto singled in the fourth.
Clement ranged to his right on a grounder from Dodgers outfielder Andy Pages."},{"url":"https://www.mlb.com/video/nl-starters-for-the-2026-all-star-game","title":"NL starters for the 2026 All-Star Game | 07/05/2026","content":"MLB.comhttps://www.mlb.com · Jul 5, 2026MLB.comhttps://www.mlb.com · Jul 5, 20264:01Drake Baldwin, Freddie Freeman, Juan Soto and Shohei Ohtani highlight the starters for the NL in the 2026 All-Star Game.
